steps.title: Find the sentence break
steps.content: One independent clause ends after "adjustments," and another begins with "Prior to VisiCalc's release." The blank must mark that sentence boundary.

steps.title: Punctuate the two clauses
steps.content: A fuses the sentences, B makes a comma splice, and D creates the awkward construction "and prior." A period cleanly separates the complete sentences. The answer is C.
